description: Before everyone fishes, there are 100 tons of fish in the lake.
Given the current situation, if everyone fishes more than 10 tons, the lake population will shrink next month.
John wanted to catch 10 tons of fish, and caught 10 tons.

The mayor and the fishermen discussed their successful adherence to a sustainable fishing plan, emphasizing the importance of continued cooperation and open communication to maintain the lake's health and their livelihoods.
conversation: Mayor

Ladies and gentlemen, let me give you the monthly fishing report. John caught 10 tons of fish. Kate caught 10 tons of fish. Jack caught 10 tons of fish. Emma caught 10 tons of fish. Luke caught 10 tons of fish.
John
Looks like we all stuck to the plan and kept our catches at 10 tons each. This way, the lake should stay healthy and sustainable for the long run. Great job, everyone!
Kate

Absolutely, John. It's great to see everyone cooperating for the greater good. If we keep this up, we can ensure a steady income and a healthy lake for years to come. Does anyone have any thoughts on how we can maintain this balance or any concerns about the current plan?
Emma

I agree, Kate. It's really encouraging to see us all working together. I think it's important that we continue to communicate openly each month about our catches and any changes we notice in the lake's health. If anyone has any suggestions or concerns, now would be a good time to share them.
Jack

I agree with everyone. It's crucial that we keep this balance and continue to communicate openly. If we notice any changes in the lake's health or if anyone has any concerns, we should address them immediately. Let's keep up the good work and ensure the sustainability of our fishing practices.
